tracking_model.checkpoint_rule: Every item should record its current execution state, latest review round, evidence refs, and next checkpoint.

items.source_label: 多个方案排序，默认按方案一提示词
items.urgency_reason: This node still mixes ranking rule, artifact expectation, UI display, and example behavior, so it keeps polluting solution-selection semantics.
blocker_summary: mixed ranking rule and UI semantics
items.success_condition: The ranking rule becomes a standalone contract object and the UI/example residue is removed from executable semantics.
unblock_conditions: decision-weighting.json and variant-set.json remain the stable downstream consumers
the ranking rule is isolated from UI presentation and example-only language
default variant selection is justified by ranking or override basis rather than list order
items.next_checkpoint: Audit one multi-solution round to confirm ranking_scope binds stable candidate ids and no UI ordering residue re-enters default selection.

items.urgency_reason: Shared-file coordination is already central to orchestration semantics, but the source still frames it as a tool rather than a contract surface.
blocker_summary: shared context and writeback semantics still bundled together
items.success_condition: The source concept is fully expressed through coordination and writeback contracts instead of a generic tool label.
unblock_conditions: shared-context-map.json, execution-supervision.json, and writeback-ledger.json stay the canonical downstream surface
tool-shaped language is removed from executable contract semantics
executor-interface.json keeps writable_targets inside the declared shared context contract
items.next_checkpoint: Audit one real execution-heavy round to confirm shared-context-map.json stays upstream of execution-supervision.json and writeback-ledger.json stays downstream of actual writeback evidence.

items.urgency_reason: This source branch still mixes workflow shell, loop rule, and orchestration behavior, which keeps the mother-structure semantically overloaded.
blocker_summary: workflow shell mixed with orchestration extensions
insufficient separation between core and execution semantics
items.success_condition: The core workflow shell is isolated from orchestration-heavy extensions.
unblock_conditions: the minimal workflow state chain remains stable as the core shell
orchestration-heavy semantics are kept outside the core workflow contract
items.next_checkpoint: Audit one source-aligned round to confirm extension modes attach around the shell without introducing new workflow states.

items.urgency_reason: The source phrase still mixes search action with modeling objective, which weakens resource semantics at the abstract method layer.
blocker_summary: action and artifact semantics are fused
items.success_condition: Resource scan action, output artifact, and prioritization logic become separate promotable units.
unblock_conditions: search-notes.json remains the bounded search evidence artifact
resource-graph.json remains the modeling artifact instead of being fused with the search action
resource-priority-policy.json carries explicit scan-order and prioritization logic separately from the graph artifact
items.next_checkpoint: Audit one real advanced-mode round to confirm search-notes.json, resource-priority-policy.json, and resource-graph.json stay separated in actual usage.

items.urgency_reason: The source role label is still informal, but its real semantics already exist in execution units and worker scopes.
blocker_summary: identity-centered naming instead of contract-centered naming
items.success_condition: The source node is replaced by explicit execution-unit semantics instead of informal agent identity language.
unblock_conditions: execution-unit and worker-scope semantics are named independently of the informal source label
shared-context coordination semantics are already stable enough to carry worker ownership
items.next_checkpoint: Audit one execution-heavy round to confirm execution_units, worker_scopes, and reference_context_refs remain sufficient without child-agent identity fallback.

items.urgency_reason: The source still frames memory editing as a tool rather than a memory-pruning and writeback contract family.
blocker_summary: memory editing and writeback semantics still bundled
items.success_condition: Memory editing semantics become explicit contract objects instead of remaining a generic tool concept.
unblock_conditions: memory-pruning.json and writeback-ledger.json remain the canonical downstream contracts
replacement summary behavior is separated from generic editing-tool language
memory_edit_events are audited explicitly instead of being folded into generic merge results
items.next_checkpoint: Audit one pruning-heavy round to confirm memory_edit_events, replacement_summaries, and reactivation_conditions remain aligned across memory-pruning.json and writeback-ledger.json.

items.urgency_reason: Its semantic function survives, but the exact file artifact is not part of the current stable contract surface.
blocker_summary: artifact name is historical rather than structurally required
items.success_condition: The node is either archived cleanly or revived only under an explicit compatibility requirement.
unblock_conditions: the semantic function is confirmed to survive through current core artifacts
no active portability or compatibility requirement needs the historical filename revived
items.next_checkpoint: Reopen only if a real external consumer or explicit compatibility test requires the exact markdown artifact identity.

items.urgency_reason: The source concept is preserved, but still lacks a distinctive executor-facing need beyond current branch classes.
blocker_summary: insufficient differentiation from existing branch classes
items.success_condition: Keep deferred until a real execution case proves it needs a distinct contract identity.
items.defer_reason: Current architecture already covers most of its function through existing branch classes.
unblock_conditions: a concrete executor-facing scenario appears that cannot be expressed by current branch classes
the topology difference from existing branch classes becomes contract-relevant rather than naming-only
items.next_checkpoint: Reopen only when a real execution pattern proves that detached history and non-default merge behavior cannot be expressed by current branch classes.
